key_insight: SimPhysNet combines physics-informed self-supervised learning with few-shot prototypical classification to predict laser welding penetration accurately from very limited labeled data.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical, low-label quality control for intelligent laser welding, reducing dependence on costly expert annotation while maintaining near-supervised performance.
method: Physics-informed neural network embeds molten-pool/keyhole priors into contrastive self-supervised learning.
Three image augmentation tasks improve feature robustness and generalization.
Prototypical networks use a small labeled set for final penetration-state classification.
evidence_signal: Using only 200 labeled images (about 5% of the labeled dataset), the model achieved 96.06% classification accuracy, comparable to fully supervised baselines.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the physics priors and image conditions match the target welding setup, so transfer to different materials or sensor settings may require retraining.
paper_title: A welding penetration prediction model for laser welding process based on self-supervised learning using physics-informed neural networks
